title: python rest api 2
content:
• app.py mein if __name__ == "__main__": app.run() commented out hai.

  • Aap terminal mein flask run command use kar rahe honge (ya set FLASK_APP=app.py then flask run).

  • Jab aap flask run karte ho, to Python app.py ko module ki tarah import karta hai (name "app"), na ki __main__ ki tarah.

  • Isliye user_controller.py mein from app import app chal jaata hai – kyunki "app" module already sys.modules mein exist karta hai.

  • Circular import resolve ho jaata ha



Aapki current project structure yeh hai:

text
rest-api-revision/mvc/
├── app.py
├── controller/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── user_controller.py
│   └── product_controller.py
├── model/
│   └── user_model.py

1. app.py (Flask app ka main file)

python
from flask import Flask
from controller.user_controller import user_bp

app = Flask(__name__)

# Blueprint register karo
app.register_blueprint(user_bp)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run(debug=True)

2. controller/user_controller.py (Blueprint ke saath)

python
from flask import Blueprint, request, jsonify
from model.user_model import user_Model

user_bp = Blueprint('user', __name__)
obj = user_Model()

@user_bp.route("/user/getall", methods=['GET'])
def user_getall_controller():
    return obj.user_getall_model()

3. model/user_model.py (Database query with proper error handling & JSON response)

python
import mysql.connector
from flask import jsonify, make_response

class user_Model():
    def __init__(self):
        try:
            self.con = mysql.connector.connect(
                host="localhost",
                user="root",
                password="root",
                database="rest_api_techfryday"   # aapke database ka naam
            )
            self.con.autocommit = True
            self.cur = self.con.cursor(dictionary=True)
            print("connection successful")   # terminal me dikhega
        except Exception as e:
            print("connection error:", e)

    def user_getall_model(self):
        try:
            self.cur.execute("SELECT * FROM user")   # table 'user' exist karni chahiye
            result = self.cur.fetchall()
            if len(result) > 0:
                return make_response(jsonify({"payload": result}), 200)
            else:
                return make_response(jsonify({"message": "no data found"}), 204)
        except Exception as e:
            return make_response(jsonify({"error": str(e)}), 500)

4. controller/__init__.py (optional – isko khali rakh sakte ho ya kuch bhi)

python
# Khali rakh sakte ho, ya fir:
# from .user_controller import user_bp

5. controller/product_controller.py (abhi empty hai – baad me bana lena)

python
# Future use ke liye placeholder

🗄️ Database Setup (MySQL mein yeh table create karein)

sql
CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS rest_api_techfryday;
USE rest_api_techfryday;

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    email VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    phone VARCHAR(20),
    role_id INT DEFAULT 1,
    password VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
);

-- Sample data (optional)
INSERT INTO user (name, email, phone, role_id, password) VALUES
('Meet', 'meet@example.com', '9876543210', 1, 'password123'),
('Sagar', 'sagar@example.com', '9876501234', 2, 'pass456');

🚀 Run kaise karein (2 tarike)

Option A – flask run (recommended)

powershell
cd C:\Users\meet1\OneDrive\Desktop\rest-api-revision\mvc
set FLASK_APP=app.py
flask run --debug

Option B – python app.py

powershell
cd C:\Users\meet1\OneDrive\Desktop\rest-api-revision\mvc
python app.py

Dono chalenge kyunki ab circular import nahi hai.

✅ Ab kya hoga?

  • Terminal mein connection successful dikhega (agar MySQL sahi se connect ho raha hai).

  • Browser mein http://127.0.0.1:5000/user/getall open karo → database ke saare users JSON format me dikhenge.

  • Agar table empty hai to "message": "no data found" aayega.

🔁 Old version vs New version – difference

CheezOld (working project)New (aapka revision wala)
Patternfrom app import app + @app.routeBlueprint (cleaner)
Circular importAvoided by flask runCompletely eliminated
Model methoduser_getall_model returns JSONSame, but with error handling
ScalabilityModerateBetter for large apps

Ab aapke paas naye version ka fully functional code hai. Copy-paste karke run karein – sab sahi chalega.



🔹 Cursor Kya Hota Hai? Aur Kyu Chahiye? Cursor ek agent jaisa hota hai jo DB ke andar jaake query chalata hai. Cursor create karne ke liye

Example: python CopyEdit self.cur.execute("SELECT * FROM users") self ka use karne se variable sirf ek function tak limited nahi rahta, poore class mein use ho sakta hai.(global hojata h )





import mysql.connector
class user_Model() :
  def __init__(self):
    try:
      self.con = mysql.connector.connect(
        host = "localhost",
        user = "root",
        password = "root",
        database = "rest_api_techfryday"
        )
      print("connection succesfull")
    except :
      print("some error in connection")


  def user_getall_model(self):
    self.cur.execute("SELECT * FROM user")
    return "Yaha pe hi business logic aayega"
     
  def user_signup_model(self):
    return "this is working and user signup model"
 

 

user_modal.py

import mysql.connector
from flask import make_response
class user_Model() :
  def __init__(self):
    try:
      self.con = mysql.connector.connect(
        host = "localhost",
        user = "root",
        password = "root",
        database = "rest_api_techfryday"
        )
      self.con.autocommit = True
      self.cur = self.con.cursor(dictionary = True)
      print("connection succesfull")
    except :
      print("some error in connection")


  def user_getall_model(self):
    self.cur.execute("SELECT * FROM user")
    result = self.cur.fetchall()
    if len(result)>0:
      res = make_response({"payload":result},200)
      res.headers["Access-Control-Allow-Origin"]="*"
   
      return res
    else :
      return make_response({"message":"no data found"},204)
     
  def user_signup_model(self):
    return "this is working and user signup model"
 

 

  • SELECT → Use fetchall() or � � Goal: Data ko read karna fetchone()
  • insert karenge toh ddetch all nhi chl rha hoga toh aap cursor.lastrowid use karo 


📌 MOST IMPORTANT

Cursor SQL queries chalata hai.

Aur:

dictionary=True

ki wajah se output dictionary format me aayega.

❌ Without dictionary=True

(1, "Ali", 25)

✅ With dictionary=True

{   "id":1,   "name":"Ali",   "age":25}

REST API me ye best hota hai.


2️⃣ Fetch Data

result = self.cur.fetchall()

📌 Kyu fetchall()?

Kyuki SELECT multiple rows return karta hai.

Example Output

[   {"id":1,"name":"Ali","age":20},   {"id":2,"name":"Ahmed","age":25}]

4️⃣ Response

res = make_response({"payload":result},200)

200 means success.

5️⃣ CORS Header

res.headers["Access-Control-Allow-Origin"]="*"

Frontend ko API access allow kar raha hai.

6️⃣ No Data

return make_response({"message":"no data found"},204)

204 = no content.

Bahut badhiya 👍
Ab tumhara actual user_model.py dekh ke main properly samjha sakta hoon ki kya ho raha hai aur kaise SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE implement karte hain.

Tum already sahi direction me ho 🔥

🔍 Tumhara Current Code Samjho

python
import mysql.connector
from flask import make_response

📌 Kya ho raha hai?

  • mysql.connector → Python ko MySQL se connect karne ke liye

  • make_response → Flask response bhejne ke liye

✅ Class Bana Rakhi Hai

python
class user_Model():

Ye ek model class hai.

Iska kaam:

  • Database queries handle karna

  • Data fetch karna

  • Insert/update/delete karna

✅ Constructor (__init__)

python
def __init__(self):

Ye automatically run hota hai jab object banta hai.

✅ Database Connection

python
self.con = mysql.connector.connect(
    host = "localhost",
    user = "root",
    password = "root",
    database = "rest_api_techfryday"
)

📌 Iska matlab

Python MySQL database se connect ho raha hai.

✅ Auto Commit

python
self.con.autocommit = True

Matlab:

Har query automatically save ho jayegi.

Toh baar baar:

python
conn.commit()

likhne ki zarurat nahi.

✅ Cursor

python
self.cur = self.con.cursor(dictionary=True)

📌 MOST IMPORTANT

Cursor SQL queries chalata hai.

Aur:

python
dictionary=True

ki wajah se output dictionary format me aayega.

❌ Without dictionary=True

python
(1, "Ali", 25)

✅ With dictionary=True

python
{
   "id":1,
   "name":"Ali",
   "age":25
}

REST API me ye best hota hai.

✅ Current SELECT Method

python
def user_getall_model(self):

Ye function sab users la raha hai.

🔥 Step-by-Step

1️⃣ Query Execute

python
self.cur.execute("SELECT * FROM user")

SQL query database ko bheji.

2️⃣ Fetch Data

python
result = self.cur.fetchall()

📌 Kyu fetchall()?

Kyuki SELECT multiple rows return karta hai.

Example Output

python
[
   {"id":1,"name":"Ali","age":20},
   {"id":2,"name":"Ahmed","age":25}
]

3️⃣ Check Data Exists

python
if len(result)>0:

Agar data mila toh response bhejo.

4️⃣ Response

python
res = make_response({"payload":result},200)

200 means success.

5️⃣ CORS Header

python
res.headers["Access-Control-Allow-Origin"]="*"

Frontend ko API access allow kar raha hai.

6️⃣ No Data

python
return make_response({"message":"no data found"},204)

204 = no content.

🔥 AB REAL LEARNING START

Tumne bola:

code me implement bhi krwao

Toh ab hum:

✅ INSERT

✅ UPDATE

✅ DELETE

✅ GET SINGLE USER

banayenge.

🔥 1. INSERT USER

✅ Method

python
def user_add_model(self, data):

data frontend/API se aayega.

✅ Full Code

python
def user_add_model(self, data):

    self.cur.execute(
        "INSERT INTO user(name, age, city) VALUES(%s,%s,%s)",
        (
            data["name"],
            data["age"],
            data["city"]
        )
    )

    return make_response(
        {"message":"USER CREATED SUCCESSFULLY"},
        201
    )

🔍 Samjho

SQL Query

python
INSERT INTO user(name, age, city)
VALUES(%s,%s,%s)

%s placeholders hote hain.

✅ Data Pass

python
(
   data["name"],
   data["age"],
   data["city"]
)

Ye safely values insert karta hai.

🔥 SQL Injection Safe

❌ Wrong

python
f"INSERT INTO user VALUES({name})"

Dangerous.

✅ Correct

python
VALUES(%s)

🔥 Inserted ID Kaise Milegi?

Tumhare notes me tha:

python
cursor.lastrowid

Use:

python
user_id = self.cur.lastrowid

✅ Better INSERT Version

python
def user_add_model(self, data):

    self.cur.execute(
        "INSERT INTO user(name, age, city) VALUES(%s,%s,%s)",
        (
            data["name"],
            data["age"],
            data["city"]
        )
    )

    return make_response(
        {
            "message":"USER CREATED",
            "id":self.cur.lastrowid
        },
        201
    )

🔥 2. GET SINGLE USER

✅ Code

python
def user_getone_model(self, id):

    self.cur.execute(
        "SELECT * FROM user WHERE id=%s",
        (id,)
    )

    result = self.cur.fetchone()

    if result:
        return make_response({"payload":result},200)

    else:
        return make_response({"message":"USER NOT FOUND"},404)

🔍 WHY fetchone() ?

Kyuki:

sql
WHERE id = ?

sirf ek row return karega.

⚠ Important

python
(id,)

comma MUST hai.

Tuple banane ke liye.

🔥 3. UPDATE USER

✅ Full Code

python
def user_update_model(self, data):

    self.cur.execute(
        "UPDATE user SET name=%s, age=%s, city=%s WHERE id=%s",
        (
            data["name"],
            data["age"],
            data["city"],
            data["id"]
        )
    )

    if self.cur.rowcount > 0:

        return make_response(
            {"message":"USER UPDATED"},
            200
        )

    else:
        return make_response(
            {"message":"NOTHING UPDATED"},
            404
        )

🔥 rowcount Kya Hai?

python
self.cur.rowcount

Batata hai kitni rows affect hui.

❌ fetchall() yaha invalid hai

Kyuki UPDATE data return nahi karta.

🔥 4. DELETE USER

✅ Full Code

python
def user_delete_model(self, id):

    self.cur.execute(
        "DELETE FROM user WHERE id=%s",
        (id,)
    )

    if self.cur.rowcount > 0:

        return make_response(
            {"message":"USER DELETED"},
            200
        )

    else:
        return make_response(
            {"message":"USER NOT FOUND"},
            404
        )

🔥 Why rowcount?

DELETE rows return nahi karta.

Sirf affected rows batata hai.

📌 FINAL CONCEPT CLEAR

SQLUse
SELECTfetchall() / fetchone()
INSERTlastrowid
UPDATErowcount
DELETErowcount

🔥 COMPLETE PROFESSIONAL MODEL

python
import mysql.connector
from flask import make_response

class user_Model():

    def __init__(self):

        self.con = mysql.connector.connect(
            host="localhost",
            user="root",
            password="root",
            database="rest_api_techfryday"
        )

        self.con.autocommit = True
        self.cur = self.con.cursor(dictionary=True)

    # GET ALL
    def user_getall_model(self):

        self.cur.execute("SELECT * FROM user")

        result = self.cur.fetchall()

        if len(result) > 0:

            return make_response(
                {"payload": result},
                200
            )

        else:

            return make_response(
                {"message": "NO DATA FOUND"},
                204
            )

    # GET ONE
    def user_getone_model(self, id):

        self.cur.execute(
            "SELECT * FROM user WHERE id=%s",
            (id,)
        )

        result = self.cur.fetchone()

        if result:

            return make_response(
                {"payload": result},
                200
            )

        else:

            return make_response(
                {"message": "USER NOT FOUND"},
                404
            )

    # INSERT
    def user_add_model(self, data):

        self.cur.execute(
            "INSERT INTO user(name,age,city) VALUES(%s,%s,%s)",
            (
                data["name"],
                data["age"],
                data["city"]
            )
        )

        return make_response(
            {
                "message": "USER CREATED",
                "id": self.cur.lastrowid
            },
            201
        )

    # UPDATE
    def user_update_model(self, data):

        self.cur.execute(
            "UPDATE user SET name=%s, age=%s, city=%s WHERE id=%s",
            (
                data["name"],
                data["age"],
                data["city"],
                data["id"]
            )
        )

        if self.cur.rowcount > 0:

            return make_response(
                {"message": "USER UPDATED"},
                200
            )

        else:

            return make_response(
                {"message": "USER NOT FOUND"},
                404
            )

    # DELETE
    def user_delete_model(self, id):

        self.cur.execute(
            "DELETE FROM user WHERE id=%s",
            (id,)
        )

        if self.cur.rowcount > 0:

            return make_response(
                {"message": "USER DELETED"},
                200
            )

        else:

            return make_response(
                {"message": "USER NOT FOUND"},
                404
            )

Acha question 🔥
Yehi real backend thinking hoti hai.

Tum pooch rahe ho:

DELETE ya UPDATE ke baad updated/deleted data bhi return kaise kare?

✅ IMPORTANT CONCEPT

UPDATE aur DELETE

direct data return nahi karte.

Isliye beginner approach hota hai:

✅ STEP 1 → Pehle SELECT karo

✅ STEP 2 → Data save karo

✅ STEP 3 → UPDATE/DELETE karo

✅ STEP 4 → Saved data return karo

🔥 1. DELETE KE BAAD DELETED DATA RETURN

✅ Beginner Flow

text
SELECT old row
↓
Store in variable
↓
DELETE row
↓
Return old row

✅ Code

python
def user_delete_model(self, id):

    # STEP 1 → fetch old data

    self.cur.execute(
        "SELECT * FROM user WHERE id=%s",
        (id,)
    )

    deleted_user = self.cur.fetchone()

    # STEP 2 → check exists or not

    if deleted_user:

        # STEP 3 → delete

        self.cur.execute(
            "DELETE FROM user WHERE id=%s",
            (id,)
        )

        # STEP 4 → return deleted data

        return make_response(
            {
                "message":"USER DELETED",
                "deleted_user": deleted_user
            },
            200
        )

    else:

        return make_response(
            {"message":"USER NOT FOUND"},
            404
        )

🔥 Example Output

json
{
   "message":"USER DELETED",
   "deleted_user":{
      "id":5,
      "name":"Ali",
      "age":25,
      "city":"Delhi"
   }
}

🔥 WHY THIS WORKS?

DELETE hone ke baad row permanently chali jaati hai.

Toh pehle fetch karna padta hai.

🔥 2. UPDATE KE BAAD UPDATED DATA RETURN

✅ Beginner Flow

text
SELECT old data
↓
UPDATE
↓
SELECT updated data
↓
Return both

✅ Code

python
def user_update_model(self, data):

    # STEP 1 → old data

    self.cur.execute(
        "SELECT * FROM user WHERE id=%s",
        (data["id"],)
    )

    old_data = self.cur.fetchone()

    if old_data:

        # STEP 2 → update

        self.cur.execute(
            """
            UPDATE user
            SET name=%s, age=%s, city=%s
            WHERE id=%s
            """,
            (
                data["name"],
                data["age"],
                data["city"],
                data["id"]
            )
        )

        # STEP 3 → fetch updated data

        self.cur.execute(
            "SELECT * FROM user WHERE id=%s",
            (data["id"],)
        )

        updated_data = self.cur.fetchone()

        # STEP 4 → return both

        return make_response(
            {
                "message":"USER UPDATED",
                "old_data": old_data,
                "updated_data": updated_data
            },
            200
        )

    else:

        return make_response(
            {"message":"USER NOT FOUND"},
            404
        )

🔥 Example Response

json
{
   "message":"USER UPDATED",

   "old_data":{
      "id":1,
      "name":"Ali",
      "age":20
   },

   "updated_data":{
      "id":1,
      "name":"Ali",
      "age":30
   }
}

🔥 REAL INDUSTRY THING

Beginner way:

text
SELECT → UPDATE → SELECT

Professional optimized ways:

  • RETURNING clause (PostgreSQL)

  • ORM automatic refresh

  • Transactions

  • Soft delete

  • Audit logs

BUT beginner ke liye tumhara current approach BEST hai ✅

🔥 IMPORTANT INTERVIEW POINT

❓ Why fetch before delete?

Because:

text
DELETE permanently removes row

After deletion:

python
fetchone()

kuch return nahi karega.

🔥 SAME LOGIC FOR UPDATE

Update ke baad old values overwrite ho jaati hain.

Toh old data pehle store karna padta hai.

✅ GOLDEN RULE

OperationBeginner Safe Way
DELETESELECT first
UPDATESELECT before & after

🔥 BONUS CLEAN VERSION

Agar sirf updated data return karna ho:

python
def user_update_model(self, data):

    self.cur.execute(
        "UPDATE user SET age=%s WHERE id=%s",
        (data["age"], data["id"])
    )

    self.cur.execute(
        "SELECT * FROM user WHERE id=%s",
        (data["id"],)
    )

    updated_user = self.cur.fetchone()

    return make_response(
        {
            "message":"UPDATED",
            "user":updated_user
        },
        200
    )

🔥 MOST IMPORTANT THING YOU LEARNED

Query TypeData Return?
SELECTYES
INSERTNO
UPDATENO
DELETENO

Isliye:

  • SELECT → fetch

  • INSERT → lastrowid

  • UPDATE/DELETE → rowcount OR manual SELECT

Yahi pura database response handling ka foundation hai 🚀
